Name: mov
Description: Copies {src} (an immediate or another register) into {Rd}.

Name: mvn
Description: Writes the bitwise complement of {Rm} into {Rd}.

Name: add
Description: Adds {src} (an immediate or register) into {Rd}. Also seen as add Rd, Rn, Rm and add Rd, sp/pc, #imm.

Name: adc
Description: Adds {Rm} plus the carry flag into {Rd} — the high half of an add wider than 32 bits.

Name: sub
Description: Subtracts {src} (an immediate or register) from {Rd}. Also seen as sub Rd, Rn, Rm and sub sp, #imm.

Name: sbc
Description: Subtracts {Rm} and the borrow (the inverted carry) from {Rd} — the high half of a wide subtract.

Name: neg
Description: Writes 0 - {Rm} into {Rd} — two's-complement negation.

Name: mul
Description: Multiplies {Rd} by {Rm} and keeps the low 32 bits in {Rd}.

Name: cmp
Description: Computes {Rn} - {src} and sets the condition flags without storing the result — the test before a conditional branch.

Name: cmn
Description: Sets the flags from {Rn} + {Rm} (a compare against a negated value), discarding the sum.

Name: tst
Description: Sets the flags from {Rn} AND {Rm}, discarding the result — tests whether selected bits are set.

Name: and
Description: Bitwise AND of {Rd} and {Rm}, written back to {Rd}.

Name: orr
Description: Bitwise OR of {Rd} and {Rm}, written back to {Rd}.

Name: eor
Description: Bitwise XOR of {Rd} and {Rm}, written back to {Rd}.

Name: bic
Description: Clears in {Rd} every bit that is set in {Rm} (AND with NOT {Rm}).

Name: lsl
Description: Shifts {Rd} left, vacated low bits filled with zero — a left shift by N multiplies by 2^N.

Name: lsr
Description: Shifts {Rd} right, vacated high bits filled with zero — the unsigned divide by 2^N.

Name: asr
Description: Shifts {Rd} right copying the sign bit inward — the signed divide by 2^N (rounding toward minus infinity).

Name: ror
Description: Rotates {Rd} right by {Rs} bits, wrapping the low bits back into the top.

Name: ldr
Description: Loads the 32-bit word at the bracketed address into {Rd}. A [pc, #off] form reads a constant from the nearby literal pool.

Name: ldrb
Description: Loads one byte from memory, zero-extended to 32 bits, into {Rd}.

Name: ldrh
Description: Loads a 16-bit halfword from memory, zero-extended to 32 bits, into {Rd}.

Name: ldrsb
Description: Loads one byte from memory, sign-extended to 32 bits, into {Rd}.

Name: ldrsh
Description: Loads a 16-bit halfword from memory, sign-extended to 32 bits, into {Rd}.

Name: ldsb
Description: Loads one byte from memory, sign-extended to 32 bits, into {Rd} (assembler spelling of ldrsb).

Name: ldsh
Description: Loads a 16-bit halfword from memory, sign-extended to 32 bits, into {Rd} (assembler spelling of ldrsh).

Name: str
Description: Stores the 32-bit word in {Rd} to the bracketed address.

Name: strb
Description: Stores the low byte of {Rd} to memory.

Name: strh
Description: Stores the low 16 bits of {Rd} to memory.

Name: push
Description: Pushes the listed registers onto the stack (lowest-numbered at the lowest address) — the prologue's register save, often including lr.

Name: pop
Description: Pops the listed registers off the stack — the epilogue's restore. Popping into pc (or popping the saved lr and bx-ing it) returns from the function.

Name: ldmia
Description: Loads consecutive words into the register list starting at the address in {Rn}, then advances {Rn} past them.

Name: stmia
Description: Stores the register list to consecutive words starting at {Rn}, then advances {Rn} past them.

Name: b
Description: Jumps unconditionally to {label}.

Name: bl
Description: Calls {label}: saves the return address in lr, then jumps. This is a function call.

Name: bx
Description: Jumps to the address in {Rn}, switching between ARM and Thumb from its low bit. `bx lr` is the standard function return.

Name: beq
Description: Branches to {label} when the previous compare was equal (Z flag set).

Name: bne
Description: Branches to {label} when the previous compare was not equal (Z flag clear).

Name: bcs
DescriptiveName: Branch if Carry Set (Unsigned Higher or Same)
Description: Branches to {label} when carry is set — an unsigned 'greater than or equal' after a compare.

Name: bcc
Description: Branches to {label} when carry is clear — an unsigned 'less than' after a compare.

Name: bmi
Description: Branches to {label} when the result was negative (N flag set).

Name: bpl
Description: Branches to {label} when the result was zero or positive (N flag clear).

Name: bvs
Description: Branches to {label} when signed overflow occurred (V flag set).

Name: bvc
Description: Branches to {label} when no signed overflow occurred (V flag clear).

Name: bhi
Description: Branches to {label} on an unsigned 'greater than' after a compare.

Name: bls
DescriptiveName: Branch if Unsigned Lower or Same
Description: Branches to {label} on an unsigned 'less than or equal' after a compare.

Name: bge
DescriptiveName: Branch if Signed Greater or Equal
Description: Branches to {label} on a signed 'greater than or equal' after a compare.

Name: blt
Description: Branches to {label} on a signed 'less than' after a compare.

Name: bgt
Description: Branches to {label} on a signed 'greater than' after a compare.

Name: ble
DescriptiveName: Branch if Signed Less or Equal
Description: Branches to {label} on a signed 'less than or equal' after a compare.

Name: .word
Description: A 32-bit constant in the literal pool — typically an address or large immediate that a nearby `ldr Rd, [pc, ...]` loads.

Name: .hword
Description: A 16-bit constant emitted inline — often alignment padding or a packed halfword value.

Name: .byte
Description: An 8-bit constant emitted inline in the instruction stream.
